churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Frontal view of the Jewish Community House of Bensonhurst (JCH), limestone Art Deco facade with carved inscription, a Romanesque arched portal with menorah tympanum and flanking globe lamps, behind a lamppost.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Symmetrical view of the Jewish Community House of Bensonhurst (JCH) limestone Art Deco facade with its arched menorah portal and globe lamps, a brick apartment tower rising behind to the right.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Detail of the Amos Masonic Temple (Amos-Fort Greene Lodge No. 922 F. & A.M.), showing an incised limestone name frieze, a Romanesque arched window with carved capital, and a fluted brick pilaster on tan brick.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Detail of the Amos Masonic Temple (Amos-Fort Greene Lodge No. 922) tan-brick facade with an arched limestone window surround, ornamented window lintels, a fluted pilaster, surveillance camera, and the lodge plaque.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: The green copper dome of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue (Emery Roth, 1910) rising above a receding red-brick fence wall along the sidewalk, with parked cars and rowhouses beyond.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue, a buff stone Neoclassical building with a green copper dome and arched windows, with a pedestrian walking past flowering cherry trees and an iron fence on the sidewalk.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Detail of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue showing its stone cornice, a Star of David stained-glass window, and the green copper dome with cross-topped finial, above blooming white cherry blossoms.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Upward view of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue's arched Star of David stained-glass window, stone cornice, and green copper dome against blue sky, framed by white cherry blossoms.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Close detail of the arched blue stained-glass Star of David window in the buff stone facade of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue, partly veiled by white cherry blossoms.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: The blue Star of David stained-glass window of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue glimpsed above a dense foreground spray of white cherry blossoms against blue sky.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: A carved stone cornerstone of Congregation Sons of Israel reading 'ORGANIZED 1896 / EMERY ROTH ARCHITECT' with a Star of David and Hebrew lettering, seen behind a rusted scrollwork iron fence.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: A blue 'Congregation Sons of Israel' synagogue sign mounted on the buff stone facade beside a carved stone monument column, above an iron-railed stone stair.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: The arched main entrance of Congregation Sons of Israel synagogue with carved stone overdoor, Star of David doors, a menorah-topped stained-glass window, a stone monument column, and the blue congregation sign, marked 'ERECTED 1910'.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Storefront mosque (Jamia Masjid / As-Salam Community Center) with Arabic signage and mini-split AC units mounted above a faded blue-and-white painted mural on a brick wall behind an iron fence.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Bath Beach commercial strip with a faded painted 'BATH BEACH' sign, a dry cleaning/tailoring shop, and the Al-Qur'an Center beneath a red-brick apartment building, with cars parked at the curb.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Chinese Buddhist temple with a green glazed-tile pagoda-style roofline and a maroon wall bearing a gold-and-orange 'NA MO AMITABHA' sunburst mural, with a cyclist passing on the street.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Corner of a red-brick Chinese Buddhist temple with green glazed pagoda-tile eaves, a carved gilt vertical signboard, decorative columns, and red double-door entry flanked by potted shrubs.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Red-brick Chinese Buddhist temple (No. 5601) with sweeping green glazed pagoda-tile roofs, dragon roof ornaments, a gilt blue signboard, and a red-framed arched entry with painted couplet columns.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: Former First Norwegian (carved-stone lintel) building in Bay Ridge, now Iglesia La Piedra Viva A.D., with an Art Deco brick storefront bay, red doors, and a 'Horario de Cultos' notice board.

churches-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
churches-of-brooklyn.meta: A blossoming tree in front of Sunset Park with a domed neoclassical church bell tower (likely a local Catholic parish) rising above brick storefronts and a deli/grocery at golden hour.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Elevated view down a South Brooklyn street showing a modern brick-and-glass apartment block with balconies, older brick walk-ups with fire escapes, a limestone Colonial Revival corner building, and a tan brick tower in the distance at sunset.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Elevated view over a South Brooklyn intersection near 6th Street with a limestone Colonial Revival corner building, modern brick apartments with balconies, brick walk-ups, and a tan brick residential tower at the end of the street.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Row of brownstone/limestone rowhouses with deep projecting bracketed cornices and round oriel bay windows with fire escapes, seen in raking light against a clear sky.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick rowhouse entry with twin arched openings, a white balustraded stoop and matching upper balustrade, hanging flower baskets and potted plants, behind a decorative scrolled wrought-iron fence.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Rooftop view over brick and vinyl-sided rowhouses near Dahill Road with the towers of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge visible in the distance.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick mixed-use storefronts on 86th Street housing China 1 Restaurant and A&H Market, with a vertical yellow 'Chinese Food' sign and a red awning.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Corner view from under an awning ('The Lights of Baku') toward a brick corner apartment building with arched windows, a pedestrian and a patterned doormat in foreground.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Three-story brick corner mixed-use building at West 10th Street and Avenue U with a ground-floor chiropractor and a 99-cent grocery, a pedestrian on the corner.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Same brick corner building at West 10th Street and Avenue U with the Dr. Scott Skalkin chiropractor and 99-cent grocery storefronts, seen in full sun.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick storefronts at 123 Avenue U housing Pueblita Grocery and J&M, with fan-light arched second-floor windows and an ornamented brick parapet, a pedestrian passing.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Same 123 Avenue U brick block with Pueblita Grocery and J&M storefronts, showing the carved cartouche, arched windows, and fire escape; a pedestrian walks at left.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Field of 18th/19th-century slate and brownstone headstones at Old Gravesend Cemetery beneath a large sycamore, backed by neighboring brick rowhouse party walls and a chain-link fence.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Dutch-inscribed brownstone headstone of Jacobus Emans (son of Abraham Emans and Sara Schenck, died Oct 1770) with winged soul-effigy carving at Old Gravesend Cemetery, brick rowhouses behind.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Same Jacobus Emans 1770 Dutch-language brownstone headstone with cherub carving at Old Gravesend Cemetery, viewed beneath a bare sycamore with rowhouses and a blank brick party wall behind.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Jacobus Emans 1770 brownstone headstone at Old Gravesend Cemetery with a clump of blooming daffodils in the foreground, ranks of weathered stones and brick walls behind.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brownstone headstone of Margrieta Emans (daughter of Abraham Emans and Sara Schenck, died March 1811) in Dutch script at Old Gravesend Cemetery, alongside a broken marble marker.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Granite McKane family monument (Isaac H. McKane, died Aug 1889, aged 35) with a verse epitaph and bold raised MCKANE lettering on the base at Old Gravesend Cemetery.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Old Gravesend Cemetery headstones and a carved stone memorial bench beneath a large sycamore, set against a blank brick party wall and neighboring frame houses.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brownstone headstone with winged soul-effigy for the Van Brunt family (Dutch inscription, dated Jan 1771) at Old Gravesend Cemetery, rows of stones and neighborhood rooftops behind the fence.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Paired Van Brunt brownstone headstones at Old Gravesend Cemetery, one with a winged cherub and Dutch text dated 1771 and another to a young Van Brunt died 1782, rows of stones along the brick boundary wall.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick rowhouse entrances with shell-motif fanlight ornaments above the doors, corrugated metal awnings, and white scrollwork wrought-iron railings over a daffodil bed.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Detail of a brick rowhouse entrance with a carved stone shell ornament, metal mailbox labeled with hand-lettered delivery instructions, and a scrollwork metal awning frame.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick two-story buildings along a rear yard with a whitewashed garage, corrugated awning, exterior stair to a deck, and chain-link fencing under a clear sky.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Spring streetscape with a cherry tree in full white bloom shading a brick rowhouse, a corner stop sign and street sign, and a person on a scooter on the sidewalk.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick rowhouses with green-striped awnings and brick stoops at the corner of Gravesend Neck Road and West Street, with one-way and stop signs and passing cars.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Dark brick building facade with a single flowering cherry tree, a parked white cargo van, a fire hydrant, and an open rooftop deck structure beyond a brick parapet wall.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Brick rowhouse (no. 2210) with a broken-pediment door surround, white scrollwork iron railings on a brick stoop, corrugated awnings, and a ground-floor garage.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY
rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Two-story brick rowhouse with a stepped parapet, enclosed front porch with metal awning and scrollwork ironwork, a flowering pink ornamental tree, and dense overhead utility wires.

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.title: Brooklyn, NY

rowhouses-of-brooklyn.meta: Red-brick rowhouses with rounded bay-window turrets and stone caps along 60th Street, with the yellow JZ Laundromat storefront (883 60th St) at right and a modern apartment block behind.
